kwargs.agent_config_yaml:
name: react
architecture: react
description: ReAct Terminal-Bench baseline agent.
model: gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview
max_turns: 30
max_tokens: 8192
tools:
- bash
- read_file
- write_file
- edit_file
- glob
- grep
components: []
definition:
  subagent_type: react
  description: Baseline ReAct workflow for Harbor and Terminal-Bench style tasks.
  runner: yaml_workflow
  color: green
  permissions: []
  plan_mode_required: false
  allow_permission_prompts: false
  skills: []
  required_mcp_servers: []
  background: false
prompts: {}
subagents:
  thinker:
    name: thinker
    architecture: simple
    description: ''
    model: gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview
    max_turns: 30
    max_tokens: 8192
    tools: []
    components: []
    prompts:
      system: "You are the reasoning step of a ReAct agent. You will be given a task\n\
        and the history of previous (reasoning, action, observation) steps\nfrom a\
        \ task environment. Decide what to do next.\n\nPopulate the response fields\
        \ as follows:\n- `reasoning`: a short paragraph analysing the current state.\n\
        - `action`: a single concrete action to take next, expressed as a\n  shell\
        \ command or as a tool invocation in the form\n  `tool_name(arg=value, ...)`.\
        \ Leave empty only when no further\n  action is needed.\n- `is_finished`:\
        \ true only when the task is verifiably complete.\n  You may set `is_finished=true`\
        \ together with a non-empty `action`:\n  the runtime will execute that final\
        \ action (e.g. writing the\n  required output file) and *then* finish. Do\
        \ not claim a side\n  effect was performed unless either the action history\
        \ shows it\n  or you provide the action that performs it on this same turn.\n\
        - `final_answer`: the final answer to surface to the caller, set\n  only when\
        \ `is_finished` is true.\n"
      user: '# Task

        {{ instruction }}


        {% if observations %}

        # Previous Steps

        {% for obs in observations %}

        ## Step {{ obs.step }}

        - Reasoning: {{ obs.reasoning }}

        - Action: {{ obs.action }}

        - Observation: {{ obs.observation }}

        {% endfor %}

        {% else %}

        This is the first step.

        {% endif %}


        Decide what to do next.

        '
    subagents: {}
  actor:
    name: actor
    architecture: simple
    description: ''
    model: gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview
    max_turns: 30
    max_tokens: 8192
    tools:
    - bash
    - read_file
    - write_file
    - edit_file
    - glob
    - grep
    components: []
    prompts:
      system: "{{ openharness_system_context }}\n\n# Actor Persona\nYou are an executor\
        \ running inside a task environment with no human\non the other end. The instruction\
        \ you receive is the action to\ncarry out \u2014 execute it directly using\
        \ the available tools.\n\nRules:\n- Always invoke a tool to make progress.\
        \ Never reply with a\n  conversational question, clarification request, or\
        \ \"please\n  provide more context\" \u2014 the caller cannot answer.\n- If\
        \ the requested action is a shell command (anything starting\n  with a binary\
        \ name, ending with `> file`, `| cmd`, etc.),\n  run it via the `bash` tool\
        \ verbatim.\n- Use non-interactive shell commands only.\n- Prefer filesystem\
        \ inspection before edits and keep changes minimal.\n- When a tool returns\
        \ no output or an empty result, that is a\n  valid observation \u2014 report\
        \ it as such and continue, do not\n  ask whether to proceed.\n"
      user: '{{ instruction }}

        '
    subagents: {}
